primary_source: The duration-only OmniVoice phrase and score timeline are reconciled by post-hoc SoulX content-hidden remapping; rapid mode holds one phone-center hidden vector at full strength. This destroys natural within-phone motion and does not provide score-native note/phoneme generation.
secondary_sources: SoulX SVC is seed-sensitive on rapid and large-interval material.
Explicit F0 conditioning does not force source articulation and note timing to agree.
The residual acoustic refiner cannot recover missing or wrong content/timbre structure.
required_next_probe: A score-native phrase SVS acoustic path whose phoneme durations, note onsets, voicing and F0 are direct model inputs; validate rapid and large intervals before GYU adaptation.
